label: Fortinet FortiOS Critical RCE (CVE-2026-12345)
summary: A critical remote code execution vulnerability in Fortinet FortiOS (CVE-2026-12345, CVSS 9.8) is being actively exploited. CISA has added it to the KEV catalog. Patches are available in FortiOS 7.4.5+.
why_it_matters: FortiOS is deployed across 500K+ enterprises globally. Active exploitation confirmed by multiple threat intelligence sources. Unauthenticated RCE allows full device takeover, making this a top-priority patching event.

articles.title: CISA Warns of Critical Fortinet FortiOS RCE Vulnerability
articles.desc: CISA has added CVE-2026-12345 to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog.
articles.author: CISA

articles.title: Active Exploitation of Fortinet FortiOS RCE Confirmed by Multiple Sources
articles.desc: Multiple threat intelligence firms confirm active exploitation of CVE-2026-12345 in the wild.
articles.author: Sergiu Gatlan

articles.title: Fortinet Releases Emergency Patch for Critical FortiOS Vulnerability
articles.desc: Fortinet has issued FortiOS 7.4.5 to address the critical RCE vulnerability CVE-2026-12345.
articles.author: FortiGuard Labs

articles.title: Technical Analysis: Fortinet FortiOS SSL-VPN RCE (CVE-2026-12345)
articles.desc: Deep dive into the root cause, exploitation mechanics, and detection strategies for CVE-2026-12345.
articles.author: Rapid7 Research
